title: Git Dirty Check: warn before running agent on uncommitted changes
description:
Why: Launching Claude/Codex against a dirty working tree can mix the user's manual edits with generated changes and make sessions harder to trust.
What:
- Check the Git working tree before launching executor-style agent actions.
- Show a clear warning when uncommitted changes exist.
- Let the user cancel or continue anyway.
- Reuse ShellCommandRunner and explicit argument arrays for git commands.
Out of scope:
- Auto-commit, auto-stash, or discarding changes.
- Blocking read-only actions like Copy Prompt unless the UX already treats it as execution.
- Breaking project folder validation.
design:
Implementation notes:
- Add a GitService or GitStatusService that runs git status --porcelain from the project root.
- Optionally read git branch --show-current and git log -1 --pretty=format:%h%x20%s for context.
- Model the result with a GitStatusSummary and GitChangedFile type.
- Use the dirty-state result to gate executor launch actions such as Implement with Selected Agent and Open Selected Agent.
- Keep the warning human-friendly and path-safe for folders that include spaces.
- Do not shell out with concatenated command strings.
acceptance_criteria:
- App can detect a clean git working tree.
- App can detect a dirty git working tree.
- Dirty check runs before executor agent launch.
- Dirty warning lists changed files.
- User can cancel.
- User can continue anyway.
- Dirty check does not auto-commit, stash, or discard changes.
- Dirty check does not break project folder validation.
- Dirty check works with paths containing spaces.
- Dirty check uses ShellCommandRunner and does not run arbitrary shell strings.
notes: Implemented git dirty-check preflight for executor launches. Added GitStatusService/GitStatusSummary parsing git status, branch, and last-commit context; wired AppViewModel to block launch on dirty worktrees with a modal warning sheet; preserved copy-only actions and existing launch history flow; surfaced probe failures in the status bar as launch errors. Verified with swift test and xcodebuild -project BeadsKanbanApp.xcodeproj -scheme BeadsKanbanApp -configuration Debug build.

title: Agent Run History: record each selected-agent execution attempt
description:
Why: Users need an app-local trail of selected-agent runs so they can review vibe-coding sessions, prompts, outcomes, and whether a run still needs review.
What:
- Record every execution attempt started from actions like Implement with Selected Agent.
- Capture issue ID, issue title, agent profile, agent name, command, prompt, project path, started/completed timestamps, status, and notes.
- Persist records locally as app metadata, not in Beads source-of-truth data.
- Surface a simple recent-runs UI section so users can inspect and update run status.
- Allow manual status changes to needsReview, accepted, failed, and abandoned.
Out of scope:
- Live terminal monitoring.
- AI API integration.
- Automatic detection of Claude/Codex completion.
design:
Implementation notes:
- Add AgentRunRecord and AgentRunStatus models.
- Add a local AgentRunHistoryStore backed by UserDefaults or a JSON file.
- Create a run record when an agent action is launched; status can start as prepared or terminalOpened depending on the UI flow.
- Reuse existing prompt generation and terminal launch behavior.
- Keep the UI intentionally small at first: recent run list with issue, agent, status, and started time.
- Avoid coupling this history to .beads mutation or issue state.
acceptance_criteria:
- A run record is created when the user starts Implement with Selected Agent.
- The record includes issue ID, issue title, agent name, command, prompt, project path, startedAt, and status.
- Records persist after app restart.
- User can see recent agent runs in the app.
- User can update run status manually.
- Existing Beads issue loading still works.
- Existing agent prompt generation still works.
- No AI API integration is added.
- No .beads files are edited directly.
notes: Implemented local Agent Run History persistence and launch tracking. Added AgentRunRecord/AgentRunStatus, file-backed AgentRunHistoryStore, and AgentRunLaunchCoordinator so the existing selected-agent launch flow records prepared -> terminalOpened/failed states with prompt, command, project path, timestamps, and notes. Wired Debug Panel to show recent agent runs with manual status updates. Verified with swift test and xcodebuild Debug build.

title: Rework WelcomeView to surface workspace context
description:
Why: The pre-workspace home screen should feel like part of the app, not a generic utility panel. It needs to communicate folder context, root discovery, validation state, and setup readiness in a way that matches the dark, premium, information-dense workstation style used elsewhere in the app.
What:
- Rebuild WelcomeView as a dark workspace dashboard using WorkstationTheme colors, borders, spacing, and typography.
- Add a branded hero/header plus compact status chips that summarize the selected folder, discovered root, validation state, and available setup hints.
- Reorganize the page into card-based sections for workspace context, validation, recent projects, and command history.
- Surface next-step guidance more clearly for empty, non-Beads, and partially configured workspaces.
- Preserve folder selection, reload validation, cancel loading, and recent-project open behavior.
- Keep the scope limited to the pre-workspace home/welcome state only.
Style guide notes:
- Use dark surfaces (background/surface/card) with warm gold accents and subtle borders.
- Keep the layout compact, premium, and easy to scan.
- Prefer rounded 8-10px cards, 10px chips, and low-noise metadata.
- Avoid bright or playful treatments; keep motion subtle.
Acceptance criteria:
- WelcomeView shows the current folder, discovered root, validation status, setup hints, and recent projects when available.
- Empty, invalid, loading, and ready states are visually distinct and easy to scan.
- Folder selection, validation reload, and recent project opening still work.
- The page feels connected to the rest of the workstation UI rather than a generic onboarding panel.
Test plan:
- Verify no folder selected, non-Beads folder, valid workspace, and workspace with recent projects.
- Confirm selected folder, discovered root, validation state, and bd init/setup guidance remain readable.
- Run swift test and xcodebuild -project BeadsKanbanApp.xcodeproj -scheme BeadsKanbanApp -configuration Debug build after implementation.
notes: Reworked WelcomeView into a dark workstation-style home dashboard aligned with references/workstations_style_guide.md. The screen now uses WorkstationTheme surfaces, warm gold accents, compact status chips, and card-based sections for workspace context, validation, recent projects, and command history. It surfaces selected folder, discovered root, validation state, bd/setup readiness hints, and next-step guidance while preserving folder selection, reload validation, cancel, and recent-project open flows. Verification: swift test passed and xcodebuild -project BeadsKanbanApp.xcodeproj -scheme BeadsKanbanApp -configuration Debug build passed.

title: Run Agent button — one-shot trigger agent dari detail view
description:
Why: Loop saat ini: lihat issue → klik Copy Agent Command → buka Terminal → paste → run. Banyak step manual. Sebagian sudah ada (copyAgentCommand di AppViewModel) tapi belum one-shot. Tujuan: dari detail view, 1 klik = agent jalan di terminal dengan prompt pre-filled.

What:
- Tombol 'Run Agent' di IssueDetailView dekat 'Copy Prompt'/'Copy Command' yang sudah ada.
- Klik → dropdown pilih AgentProfile (Claude Executor / Codex Executor / dll).
- App execute: TerminalLauncher.openTerminal(at: workspacePath, command: generatedCommand).
- Generated command pakai PromptGenerator.generateCommand yang sudah ada.
- Kalau profile.shouldClaimIssue=true, auto-trigger 'bd update --claim --assignee=<kind>' sebelum launch terminal (parallel optional, tapi minimal urut).
- Settle ke status in_progress di app secara optimistic, file watcher confirm.

Out of scope:
- Embed terminal di app (terlalu kompleks, security).
- Stream output dari terminal balik ke UI (separate issue 3.2 Watch Mode).
- Konfigurasi terminal apps selain default (Terminal.app/iTerm).
acceptance_criteria: (1) Klik 'Run Agent' → menu profile muncul. (2) Pilih 'Claude Code Executor' → Terminal.app terbuka di workspace dir, command ter-fill, issue otomatis status=in_progress + assignee=claude di kanban. (3) Error launch terminal → terminalErrorMessage surface ke StatusBar (existing). (4) Profile dengan shouldClaimIssue=false skip auto-claim. (5) Test BeadsService.claim sudah covered; PromptGenerator existing test cukup.
notes:
Hardening: codex Run Agent sekarang buka TUI interactive, bukan exec mode.

Problem: 'codex exec "<prompt>"' itu non-interactive — codex cuma stream output mentah (reasoning + diff) ke terminal tanpa TUI. Saat agent jalan, user susah bedain 'lagi mikir' vs 'udah selesai' vs 'lagi nunggu input'. Screenshot user nunjukin terminal penuh diff text dengan footer 'tokens used' — TUI codex ga muncul sama sekali.

Root cause: AgentProfile builtIn untuk Codex (specWriter & codingExecutor) pakai commandArgsTemplate = 'exec "{{prompt}}"'. Codex CLI dengan subcommand 'exec' = scripting mode (no TUI). Tanpa subcommand = interactive TUI dengan prompt sebagai positional arg (verified via 'codex --help': 'If no subcommand is specified, options will be forwarded to the interactive CLI').

Fix (2 baris):
- Sources/BeadsContract/AgentProfile.swift: kedua codex builtIn profile (specWriterID + codexExecutorID) ganti commandArgsTemplate dari 'exec "{{prompt}}"' → '"{{prompt}}"'.
- Tests/BeadsContractTests/PromptGeneratorTests.swift: update generateCommandCodexExecutor — expect 'codex "' prefix, dan tambah negative assertion !hasPrefix('codex exec').

Yang TIDAK berubah:
- claude profile: sudah interactive ('"{{prompt}}"'), tidak disentuh.
- TerminalLauncher / AppleScript flow: tetap.
- Custom user profiles yang manual set 'exec' ke commandArgsTemplate: tetap dihormati (cuma builtIn yang berubah). AgentProfileStore.loadProfiles() me-rebuild builtIn dari kode, jadi user dapat update otomatis di app restart.

Verifikasi:
- swift test: 213/213 hijau (baseline 213, tidak ada test baru).
- ./run-app build: BUILD SUCCEEDED.
- Manual smoke pending: jalankan Run Agent → Codex Code Executor di issue manapun. Expect: Terminal.app buka, 'cd <path> && codex "<prompt>"' dieksekusi, codex TUI muncul (split view, model picker di bawah, input area di atas), prompt udah tertulis sebagai initial user message. Bukan lagi diff text streaming.

Out of scope per problem statement:
- Banner echo sebelum agent start (ditahan dulu — fix utama udah cukup, banner bisa ditambah kalau ambiguity masih ada di kasus lain).
- TerminalLauncher error capture (osascript exit code surfacing) — issue terpisah, scope hardening.
- iTerm fallback.

title: Filter bar di header — priority/type/assignee/label
description:
Why: Begitu workspace > 20 issue board jadi sesak dan sulit fokus ke subset relevan. Saat ini cuma bisa filter dengan scroll/search via Cmd+F (yang juga belum ada). Filter bar persistent = setiap user lihat board sudah ter-scope ke konteks mereka.

What:
- Filter bar di bawah HeaderView, chip-row horizontal.
- Filter dimensions: Priority (P0-P4), Type (task/bug/feature/epic/chore), Assignee (resolved kinds: Claude/Codex/Other/me), Label (dynamic dari union semua label di issues).
- Multiple chip aktif dalam dimensi sama = OR; antar dimensi = AND.
- Clear-all button di kanan bar saat ≥1 filter aktif.
- State persist per workspace di AppPreferences (extend dengan filterState: [workspaceID: FilterState]).
- IssueStore compute filteredIssues; semua view (kanban/list) konsumsi yang sudah filtered.

Out of scope:
- Saved filter presets.
- Custom filter logic (NOT/regex). Cukup multi-select per dimensi.
- Filter di Done column (Done sudah ada doneVisibilityWindow).
acceptance_criteria: (1) Filter chip 'P0' aktif → hanya card P0 visible di semua kolom. (2) Filter 'P0' + 'P1' aktif → card P0 atau P1 visible. (3) Filter 'P0' + 'type=bug' → card P0 dan bug. (4) Clear-all reset semua, board penuh lagi. (5) Restart app → filter aktif sebelumnya tetap aktif (persist via AppPreferences). (6) Test IssueStore.filteredIssues untuk 4 kombinasi filter.
notes: follow-up: replaced the inline chip strip with a single Filter button in the header that opens a popover form containing the same priority/type/assignee/label controls; kept persistence and filtering logic unchanged.

title: Ganti monogram assignee badge ke SF Symbol per agent
description:
Why: Saat ini AssigneeBadgeView render monogram text ('CL'/'CX') di kotak/kapsul berwarna. Recognisability rendah — user susah scan cepat 'mana issue Claude vs Codex' di kanban dense. SF Symbol dengan warna brand lebih instant-recognizable dan tetap konsisten sama Craftboard style (stroke icon, dark surface, accent tint).

What:
- .claude → SF Symbol 'sparkles', warna WorkstationTheme.accent (gold #ECC864), bg Color(hex 1A1608), border 3A2F0A. Capsule shape dipertahankan.
- .codex → SF Symbol 'chevron.left.forwardslash.chevron.right', warna WorkstationTheme.blue, bg 0F1A1F, border 0F2535. Rounded-rect shape dipertahankan.
- .initials → tetap text monogram (no change) — untuk human assignee fallback.
- Compact + normal size keep working.

Why opsi 1 (SF Symbol) bukan asset PNG:
- Anthropic/OpenAI brand guideline melarang modifikasi logo resmi.
- Zero asset = no Assets.xcassets bloat, no @1x/@2x/@3x management.
- SF Symbol auto-scale + auto-tint mengikuti color scheme.
- Tradeoff: less 'brand-instant' tapi konsisten sama style guide Craftboard (stroke iconography section 10).

Out of scope:
- Mengubah AgentProfile.avatarMonogram (tetap ada untuk fallback/tooltip).
- Picker 'Claim for Claude / Codex' di detail view — separate UX issue.
- Mengganti AvatarKind enum — cuma swap rendering di view layer.
notes: Swap monogram text → SF Symbol per agent kind di App/AssigneeBadgeView.swift avatarGlyph(): .claude = 'sparkles' (gold accent, capsule preserved), .codex = 'chevron.left.forwardslash.chevron.right' (blue, rounded-rect preserved), .initials = unchanged (text monogram untuk human fallback). Background/border/shape per kind tidak berubah — pure rendering swap. accessibilityLabel ditambah ('Claude'/'Codex') untuk screen reader. Compact dan normal size scale via iconSize. swift test AssigneeAvatarResolver 3/3 hijau; xcodebuild SUCCEEDED. 2 failure di BeadsServiceTests (label/assignee decode) tidak terkait — pre-existing dari decoder tolerant work sebelumnya, perlu issue terpisah. Not closing — flagged for human review per executor convention.

title: Phase 10.4 — Craftboard workspace UI refresh
description:
Why: The current workspace UI still feels like a native split-view utility and the preferred direction is the Craftboard dark productivity workspace from references/workstation_ui_ux.md and references/workstations_style_guide.md.

What to do:
- Replace the active workspace feel with a custom Craftboard-style shell: fixed dark sidebar, top header/tabs, center Kanban/List content, and right issue detail drawer.
- Add shared Craftboard tokens for charcoal surfaces, thin borders, muted text, compact spacing/radius, and warm gold active states.
- Restyle Kanban metrics, lanes, issue cards, selected state, and detail drawer while preserving existing Beads data/action behavior.
- Keep welcome/debug/sheets functional but not fully redesigned in this pass.

Out of scope: new Beads commands, model changes, persistence changes, full onboarding/debug/modal polish.
acceptance_criteria:
- Active workspace uses the Craftboard-inspired custom shell instead of the native split-view look.
- Kanban/List tabs still switch modes.
- Card selection opens the right detail drawer.
- Claim, review, close, reopen, reload, new issue, debug, terminal, copy prompt, and copy command behaviors still work.
- swift test and ./run-app build pass.
- Issue is left open with human label for visual review.
notes: Implemented Craftboard-style workspace refresh: replaced native split-view shell with fixed dark sidebar, top header/tabs, center content, and right detail drawer; added shared WorkstationTheme tokens and hex color helper; restyled Kanban metrics, dot board background, lightweight lanes, compact issue cards, assignee badges, detail properties/actions, and status bar. Preserved existing Beads data flow and action wiring. Verified with swift test and ./run-app build. Leaving open for human visual review.

title: Auto-reload issues — file watcher + window focus hook
description:
Why: Saat ini user harus klik tombol Reload manual setiap kali ada perubahan di sisi `bd` (mis. ngerjain via CLI di terminal lain, atau agent Claude/Codex update issue). Workflow jadi kaku dan gampang ketinggalan info. `bd` mempassive-export ke `.beads/issues.jsonl` di setiap mutation, jadi file system watching feasible.

What:
- **File watcher**: Pakai `DispatchSource.makeFileSystemObjectSource` (atau `FSEventStream` kalau butuh recursive) di `Sources/BeadsWorkspace/` untuk watch `<workspace>/.beads/issues.jsonl`. Saat file berubah → debounce 300ms → trigger `store.reload()`. Debounce penting biar burst write (mis. close banyak issue sekaligus) ga spam reload.
- **Window focus**: Di `App/` (App lifecycle atau ScenePhase), reload saat `scenePhase == .active` (kembali dari background). Skip kalau watcher udah fire dalam X detik terakhir untuk hindari double-reload.
- **Lifecycle**: Watcher harus dibuat saat workspace di-set, di-cancel saat workspace berubah/closed. Cleanup di deinit.
- **Preferences toggle (opsional)**: `AppPreferences.autoReloadEnabled: Bool` (default true). User bisa matiin kalau ganggu. Polling interval sengaja NOT ditambahin — file watcher udah cukup.
- **Fail-soft**: Kalau file watcher gagal init (permission, path ga ada), log dan fall back ke focus-hook saja. Jangan crash.

Out of scope:
- Push notification dari bd ke app (bd ga punya event bus).
- Granular reload (cuma issue yang berubah) — full reload aja cukup untuk dataset segini.
- Background polling — focus-hook udah cover kasus app inactive.
acceptance_criteria:
- Ubah issue dari CLI di terminal lain → kanban app auto-refresh dalam <1 detik tanpa klik apa-apa.
- Reload window saat user kembali fokus ke app dari aplikasi lain.
- Debounce mencegah lebih dari 1 reload per 300ms saat ada burst write.
- File watcher di-teardown saat workspace ganti / app close (tidak leak).
- Tests: watcher fire reload, debounce works, fail-soft saat path invalid.
- Toggle preference `autoReloadEnabled` ada (default on) dan persisten.
notes: Additional UI fixes applied on top of existing auto-reload implementation: (1) IssueDetailView text overflow fix — added .fixedSize(horizontal: false, vertical: true) to description/acceptance/notes text sections so long content wraps properly instead of clipping. (2) Dependency chips now stack vertically (VStack) with full-width layout for proper text truncation of long titles. (3) Added notes field to BeadIssue model (property, CodingKey, decoder) — was present in bd JSON but missing from Swift model. (4) IssueDetailView now shows Notes section when present. (5) All 170+ tests pass, xcodebuild SUCCEEDED.

title: Surface bd dependencies di Kanban app (foundation + display)
description:
Why: `bd` punya native dependency support (`bd dep add`, `bd blocked`, `bd show` tampilkan blockers/blocked-by), tapi UI Kanban app saat ini sama sekali tidak surface info itu. Akibatnya: kolom Blocked cuma terisi kalau issue di-set `status=blocked` manual — issue yang depend on issue lain yang belum kelar tetap nyangkut di Backlog/Ready tanpa indikator. User ga punya visibility kenapa suatu issue belum bisa dikerjain.

What:
- Tambah `bd blocked --json` fetch di BeadsService → expose list ID yang blocked-by-dependency.
- Tambah `BeadsService.showIssue(id:)` pakai `bd show <id> --json` yang return blockers/blocked-by (atau pakai `bd dep list` kalau lebih cocok — research dulu).
- Update BeadIssue / bikin struct DependencyInfo (blockerIDs, blockedByIDs) opsional di model.
- IssueStore: simpan blockedIDs set, expose via computed atau attach ke issue.
- KanbanStateMapper: kalau issue `status=open` dan ada di blockedIDs → route ke .blocked (bukan backlog/ready). Closed dan label human tetap menang.
- IssueDetailView: tampilkan section 'Blocked by' (chip per ID, click → navigate ke issue itu) dan 'Blocks' (dependents).
- IssueCardView: tambah icon kecil kalau punya blocker open (e.g. `exclamationmark.triangle.fill` orange).

Out of scope: add/remove dependency dari UI (akan jadi issue terpisah).
acceptance_criteria:
- BeadsService punya method buat fetch blocked list dan dependency tree dari bd.
- Issue yang punya open blocker otomatis route ke kolom Blocked di Kanban (tidak perlu status=blocked manual).
- IssueDetailView nampilkan 'Blocked by' dan 'Blocks' dengan ID issue (minimal text, idealnya clickable).
- IssueCardView punya indikator visual kecil kalau ada open blocker.
- Tests di contract + workspace cover: mapping ke .blocked via dependency, service fetch parsing, fail-soft kalau bd command gagal.
notes:
Surfaced bd dependencies in Kanban app (foundation + display). Implementation:

Model: BeadIssue gains 3 optional fields (blockedBy, dependencies, dependents) wired through CodingKeys (blocked_by, dependencies, dependents). Backward-compat via synthesized Codable on optionals — legacy JSON still decodes.

Service: BeadsService.blockedIssues() invokes 'bd blocked --json'.

Store: IssueStore parallel-fetches bd blocked alongside list/ready/closed in performReload. Populates blockedByDependencyIDs (Set) and blockersMap (id -> [blockerIDs]). Fail-soft via fetchBlocked() — if bd blocked errors, kanban falls back to status-based mapping. Adds selectIssue(id:) detail fetch: kicks off 'bd show <id>' Task, cancels previous; populates selectedIssueDetail with dependencies/dependents nested arrays. clearSelection resets detail.

Mapper: KanbanStateMapper.column gains blockedIDs: Set<String> = []. Order: closed > human label > dependency-blocked > status switch. Default value keeps existing callers/tests intact.

UI: KanbanBoardView passes store.blockedByDependencyIDs.contains(issue.id) to IssueCardView, which renders a red exclamation-triangle 'Blocked' badge in tagRow. IssueDetailView adds dependenciesSection (between propertiesSection and textSections) showing 'Blocked by' and 'Blocks' chip groups using a private FlowHStack Layout for wrap; chips are Buttons that call store.selectIssue(id:) for fast cross-issue navigation.

Tests: 156 passing total. Added 5 mapper tests (blockedIDs routing & precedence), 3 BeadIssueTests (decode/legacy/roundtrip of dependency fields), 2 BeadsService tests (blocked args + empty), 5 IssueStore tests (blocked state, blocked-column routing, fail-soft, selectIssue->detail, clearSelection->reset).

Verification:
- swift test: 156/17 suites green
- swift build: clean
- xcodegen generate: ok
- ./run-app build: BUILD SUCCEEDED

Out of scope per plan (deferred): add/remove dependency via UI (Workstation-3s3), blocker indicator in IssueListView, live auto-refresh (Workstation-pb5).

title: Phase 10.3c — Kanban visual QA and responsive hardening
description:
Why: After the visual polish and dashboard metrics land, the kanban board needs a final pass for edge cases that can make polished UI feel fragile in real use.

What to do:
- Check long issue titles, long assignee names, narrow columns, empty board states, and high issue counts.
- Add or refine accessibility labels where board polish introduced custom UI.
- Verify selected, hover/focus, loading, and empty states remain legible.
- Run final Swift tests and app build.
- Keep this pass focused on hardening, not adding new board features.
acceptance_criteria:
- Long titles and assignee badges do not overflow card bounds.
- Empty and high-count columns remain readable.
- Custom board UI has useful accessibility labels where needed.
- swift test passes.
- ./run-app build passes.
- Any remaining visual follow-up is captured in Beads instead of being left implicit.
notes:
Visual QA and responsive hardening pass completed across 4 view files:

IssueCardView: removed fixedSize that conflicted with lineLimit(3), constrained AssigneeBadge maxWidth to 160 in footer (prevents long names pushing date off-screen), added accessibilityLabel/accessibilityAddTraits for card+selection state.

KanbanBoardView: added accessibilityLabel to board container, column headers ('Ready column, 3 issues'), and empty states ('No issues in Backlog').

IssueDetailView: added lineLimit(4)+fixedSize(h:false v:true) to title for proper wrapping without dominating panel, added lineLimit(1)+truncationMode to updated timestamp, added accessibilityLabel to title section.

IssueListView: added layoutPriority(1) to row title and layoutPriority(-1) to badge row so title always gets space first, constrained AssigneeBadge to maxWidth 140, added accessibilityLabel+accessibilityAddTraits to rows/section headers/empty states.

swift test all passed, xcodebuild BUILD SUCCEEDED. Leaving open for human visual review.

title: Phase 10.3b — Add Kanban dashboard summary metrics
description:
Why: After the base kanban visual polish, the board should gain a compact dashboard-style summary layer that gives quick project status at a glance without changing issue data.

What to do:
- Add a compact metric strip above the kanban columns.
- Derive metrics from existing IssueStore counts only.
- Include active/open, ready, in progress, review, and done counts.
- Keep the visual treatment restrained and native-app appropriate.
- Do not add new Beads commands, data model fields, or state mapping behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- Kanban mode shows a compact summary metric strip above the columns.
- Metrics reflect current IssueStore data.
- The metric strip does not crowd the board or break horizontal scrolling.
- List mode and detail pane behavior remain unchanged.
- Existing tests and app build pass.
notes: Implemented compact Kanban dashboard summary metrics above the board: Active counts visible non-Done work, plus Ready, In Progress, Review, and Done counts from existing IssueStore column accessors. Styled with the existing Kravio dark KanbanTheme and kept behavior/data flow unchanged. Verified with swift test and ./run-app build. Leaving open for human review.

title: Phase 10.3a — Polish Kanban columns and issue cards
description:
Why: The kanban board works, but the columns and cards still feel utilitarian. This first polish pass should improve the main board scanning experience without expanding scope into dashboard metrics.

What to do:
- Improve kanban card spacing, typography, and selected state.
- Refine difficulty badge styling and assignee badge placement.
- Make column headers, counts, and empty states easier to scan.
- Preserve existing board behavior, issue selection, detail pane behavior, and kanban data mapping.
- Avoid unrelated architectural refactors.
acceptance_criteria:
- Board cards are visually cleaner and easier to scan.
- Column headers and count badges feel intentional.
- Empty columns show a polished empty state.
- Existing kanban interactions still work.
- No issue data or state behavior changes are introduced.
notes: Implemented Phase 10.3a Kanban columns/cards polish: added Kravio-inspired dark board theme, upgraded column surfaces/headers/count capsules/empty states, and refined issue cards with stronger hierarchy, difficulty pills, assignee placement, selected state, and preserved unknown-status warning. Verified with swift test and ./run-app build. Leaving open for human visual review.

title: Phase 10.1 — Replace priority labels with difficulty labels
description:
Why: The UI currently exposes issue priority with P1/P2/P4-style labels, which is too technical for the product direction. We want the app to present a simpler difficulty/importance model instead.

What to do:
- Replace visible priority labels with user-facing labels: Medium, Low, High, Important/Must.
- Keep the underlying issue data unchanged unless a mapping layer is required.
- Update any board/list/detail UI surfaces that render the old labels.
- Preserve sorting/filters behavior unless explicitly tied to the label text.
- Add tests for the label mapping so the UI does not regress.

Acceptance criteria:
- No visible P1/P2/P4 labels remain in the main UI.
- The new label set is used consistently across list/board/detail surfaces.
- Existing issue data still loads correctly.
notes: Implemented PriorityDifficulty mapping (5-level: Must/Important/High/Medium/Low) di BeadsContract. Updated 4 UI surfaces: IssueCardView, IssueListView, IssueDetailView, CreateIssueSheet Picker. Added 10 tests di PriorityDifficultyTests (137 total hijau, was 127). priorityColor by-Int tetap utuh; data layer tidak diubah. swift test/build + xcodebuild semua hijau.

title: Phase 10.2 — Add assignee profile icons
description:
Why: Assignee display is currently text-only. We want a quick visual cue so users can see who owns an issue at a glance.

What to do:
- Show an icon/avatar next to the assignee name in issue cards and detail views.
- Use a readable fallback such as the initial 'R' for the current user/profile when no custom icon is available.
- Render Codex with a Codex logo-style mark and Claude with a Claude logo-style mark where supported by the profile metadata.
- Make the rendering agent-agnostic so future executor profiles can provide their own icon.
- Ensure the icon does not break layout in compact board cards.

Acceptance criteria:
- Assignee badges appear consistently in the board, list, and detail UI.
- The current user can be represented with an 'R' fallback when no logo is configured.
- Codex and Claude profiles show distinct icons.
- UI remains stable on narrow card widths.
close_reason: Added assignee avatar metadata and resolver, rendered assignee badges in kanban cards, list rows, and detail view, and covered Codex/Claude/initials fallback with tests.

title: Review column + bd human workflow
description: Tambah capability flag shouldRequestHumanReview di AgentProfile + kolom Review di kanban (issue dengan label 'human'). Built-in Claude/Codex Executor jadi review-mode (shouldCloseIssue=false, shouldRequestHumanReview=true). PromptGenerator template instruction: 'bd update <id> --add-label human' bukan 'bd close'. IssueStore.requestHumanReview(id:) call BeadsService.addLabel. UI: button Request Review menggantikan Close untuk executor profile.
acceptance_criteria: AgentProfile punya shouldRequestHumanReview (Codable backward-compat). KanbanColumn punya case .review. Mapper: label 'human' AND status != closed → .review. BeadsService.addLabel calls bd update --add-label. IssueStore.requestHumanReview wired. UI button Request Review visible saat capability ON, hilang otherwise. swift test ~120 hijau.
close_reason: Review column + bd human workflow shipped. AgentProfile.shouldRequestHumanReview baru (decodeIfPresent backward-compat). Built-in Claude/Codex Executor: shouldCloseIssue=false + shouldRequestHumanReview=true. KanbanColumn.review (urut: Backlog→Ready→InProgress→Review→Blocked→Done). Mapper: label 'human' AND status != closed → .review (closed wins). BeadsService.addLabel calls 'bd update --add-label'. IssueStore.requestHumanReview + reviewIssues computed. PromptGenerator executorTemplate branches on flags (review vs close vs noop). UI: Request Review button (executor only), Close jadi 'Approve & Close…' saat issue di Review, gating Close di-relax supaya human user bisa close. 8 test baru (3 mapper + 1 profile + 2 prompt + 1 service addLabel + 1 service blank-reject + 1 store), total 127 hijau. App build SUCCEEDED.

title: Done column 24h visibility window
description: Saat ini bd list --json default tidak include closed, jadi kolom Done di kanban selalu kosong. Fetch recently-closed (closed_at >= now - 24h) lalu merge ke list issues. Add preference untuk window duration (default 24h).
acceptance_criteria: BeadsService punya method listRecentlyClosed(within:). IssueStore reload merge open + recent-closed. Kanban kolom Done menampilkan issue closed dalam 24 jam terakhir. Tests cover service + store merge logic. Preference doneVisibilityWindow exposed (default 24h).
close_reason: Done column window 24h: BeadIssue tambah closedAt, BeadsService.closedIssues calls 'bd list --status=closed --json', IssueStore fetch+filter pakai injected now() + doneVisibilityWindow (default 24h from AppPreferences), fail-soft kalau bd lama tidak support flag. 5 new tests: service call shape, fresh-closed populates Done, stale filtered out, zero window skips fetch, fetch failure silent. swift test 112 hijau, app build SUCCEEDED.

title: Phase 8.2 — JSON Decoder Resilience verification
description: Verifikasi BeadsJSONDecoder ignore unknown fields dengan graceful (decodeIfPresent), surfacing raw JSON di debug panel saat decode partial-fail. PRD §8.2 menekankan: bd CLI bisa tambah field baru, app tidak boleh crash. Audit BeadIssue + decoder + add test untuk JSON dengan extra fields.
acceptance_criteria: Test JSON dengan field unknown decode sukses dan return BeadIssue valid. DebugPanel bisa menampilkan raw JSON last response. Tidak ada precondition crash dari missing optional field.
close_reason: Added decoder resilience tests for unknown fields and wrapped payloads, preserved raw JSON on BeadsError.jsonDecodeFailed via IssueStore state, and exposed the latest decode failure in the debug panel alongside command history.

title: Phase 8.1 — Expand BeadsAppError taxonomy
description: Saat ini BeadsError punya 4 case (commandFailed, jsonDecodeFailed, runnerFailure, invalidInput). PRD §8.1 mau 7 case: tambah bdNotInstalled, invalidProjectFolder, beadsNotInitialized, timeout, permissionDenied. Masing-masing punya userFacingMessage yang jelas + recovery suggestion.
acceptance_criteria: BeadsError punya 7 cases sesuai PRD. Setiap case punya localizedDescription dan recoverySuggestion. Tests cover semua case mapping.
close_reason: Expanded BeadsAppError to the PRD 8.1 taxonomy with user-facing messages and recovery suggestions, mapped bd/service/validator failures into the new cases, and added tests for every case plus the new error translations.

title: Phase 7.3 — UI hints for missing bd setup
description: Saat workspace tidak punya .beads atau bd belum diinstall, tampilkan hint actionable di UI: 'Run bd init', 'Run bd setup claude', atau 'Install bd via brew'. Saat ini WorkspaceValidator mendeteksi state tapi UI cuma blank. Lihat PRD Phase 7.3.
acceptance_criteria: UI menampilkan hint spesifik berdasarkan validationState (.bdMissing, .beadsNotInitialized). Tombol/link untuk copy command suggestion ke clipboard.
close_reason: Added actionable setup hints in the welcome validation UI for missing .beads, missing AGENTS.md, and missing bd CLI, with per-hint copy-to-clipboard commands and model tests for hint selection.

title: Phase 10: Generalize agent workflow with Codex + capability flags
description: Add built-in Codex Code Executor, capability flags (canExecuteCode/shouldClaimIssue/shouldCloseIssue), commandArgsTemplate, and new Copy Command action. Backward-compat Codable for persisted custom profiles.
close_reason: Agent workflow generalization selesai (scope di luar PRD): tambah built-in Codex Code Executor + 4 capability fields (commandArgsTemplate, canExecuteCode, shouldClaimIssue, shouldCloseIssue) backward-compat via custom Codable init. Tambah Copy Command action + button gating per capability. 104 SPM test hijau. Catatan: nomor 'Phase 10' di title misleading — bertabrakan dengan PRD §10 Phase 10 (Testing Strategy).

title: Hardening: fix B1+B2+H1+H2 before Phase 6
description: Phase 3-5.5 hardening: reload race (IssueStore), ready-status mapping (KanbanStateMapper), AgentProfileStore dedup, BeadsService id validation. Plus regression tests.
close_reason: Fixed B1 (reloadTask identity check), B2 (status=ready maps to .ready), H1 (AgentProfileStore dedup against built-in IDs and within custom list, addProfile rejects built-in overwrite & dedups by id), H2 (BeadsService validates id on show/claim/update/close/reopen). 79 tests green (+7 regression tests).

title: Phase 5.5 — Multi-Agent Foundation (backend-only)
description: Add AgentRole, AgentProfile (+built-ins), PromptGenerator in BeadsContract; AgentProfileStore (@Observable @MainActor) in BeadsWorkspace; Clipboard + TerminalLauncher in App/. ~16 new tests. No UI changes (option-a). See plan file.

title: Prompt templates: add assignee token guidance

title: Worktree Support: create isolated git worktree for agent runs
description:
Why: Some agent runs should happen in an isolated checkout so the user can protect the main working tree and explore safer or parallel workflows.
What:
- Optionally create a git worktree for a selected issue before launching an agent.
- Use a deterministic folder and branch naming scheme derived from project name and issue ID.
- Open Terminal in the worktree and run the selected agent there.
- Show the created worktree path to the user.
Dependencies:
- This issue must be blocked by Git Dirty Check.
- Do not implement this until dirty-state detection and warning behavior exist.
Out of scope:
- Automatic worktree deletion.
- Parallel session monitoring.
- Any AI API integration.
design:
Implementation notes:
- Add a WorktreeService or extend GitService.
- Use git worktree add <worktreePath> -b agent/<issueID> with explicit args.
- Before creating a worktree, verify the repo is a git repository, run the dirty check, verify the target folder does not already exist, and verify the branch name is available.
- Sanitize issue IDs for branch and folder names.
- After creation, optionally run safe Beads commands in the worktree to verify the workspace still works there.
- Keep the MVP simple and avoid auto-cleanup logic.
acceptance_criteria:
- App can create a worktree for a selected issue.
- Worktree path is based on project name and issue ID.
- Branch name is based on issue ID.
- App refuses or warns if the main working tree is dirty.
- App does not overwrite an existing worktree folder.
- App opens Terminal in the created worktree.
- App can run selected safe built-in agent command from the worktree.
- Existing normal terminal launch still works.
- No automatic deletion of worktrees is required for MVP.
- No parallel session manager is required for MVP.
notes: Hardened GitWorktreeService with 3 fixes: (1) best-effort `git worktree prune` before `worktree add` so stale `.git/worktrees/<name>` registrations from manually-deleted worktree folders don't block re-creation; (2) skip `.beads` symlink silently when source folder doesn't exist (prevents dangling links and cryptic bd failures inside worktrees); (3) rollback partial worktree + branch via `worktree remove --force` and `branch -D` if symlink step throws — prevents next attempt being stuck on worktreeFolderExists/branchAlreadyExists. Added 2 new stub-runner/real-FS tests covering prune-before-add ordering and skip-symlink-when-source-missing. Rollback path covered by code review only (deterministic FS-level symlink failure needs FileManager injection — out of scope). swift test: 215 passed. swift build + ./run-app build: BUILD SUCCEEDED.

title: Markdown rendering di description card & detail view
description:
Why: description issue sering punya struktur (heading, list, code block, link). Saat ini di-render plain text — list jadi rapuh, code block hilang formatting, link tidak clickable. Bikin reader susah scan konteks issue.

What:
- Render description di IssueDetailView pakai markdown (heading, list, code, link, bold/italic).
- Render preview description di kanban card juga (truncated, plain-ish — pertahankan emphasis tapi skip heading besar).
- Pilihan implementasi: AttributedString (native, terbatas) atau swift-markdown-ui (lebih lengkap, deps tambahan). Default: coba AttributedString dulu, fallback ke library kalau kurang.
- Link clickable buka di browser default.

Out of scope:
- WYSIWYG editor — input description tetap plain textarea.
- Image embedding di description.
- Mermaid / diagram rendering.
acceptance_criteria:
- Description di IssueDetailView render markdown (heading, bullet, numbered list, code inline & block, bold/italic, link).
- Link bisa diklik buka di browser.
- Card di kanban tampilkan preview description dengan emphasis terjaga (truncated 2-3 baris).
- Plain text description tetap render apa adanya (backward compatible).
- Test snapshot/unit untuk markdown renderer min 3 case (heading, list, code block).
- swift test hijau, xcodebuild SUCCEEDED.
notes: Implemented shared markdown rendering for issue descriptions. Added MarkdownTextRenderer in Sources/BeadsWorkspace and wired IssueDetailView + IssueCardView to render markdown-backed Text. Detail view now opens links through NSWorkspace/default browser. Preview rendering strips block presentation intents so cards stay compact and plain-ish while preserving inline emphasis. Regenerated BeadsKanbanApp.xcodeproj with xcodegen so the new source file is included in the macOS target. Verification: swift test ✅, xcodebuild -project BeadsKanbanApp.xcodeproj -scheme BeadsKanbanApp -configuration Debug -derivedDataPath .derivedData build ✅.

title: Starfield background — animated twinkling dots on Kanban board
description:
Why: The current DotsBackground on the Kanban board is a static grid of tiny dark dots. It feels flat and lifeless. Upgrading it to a starfield effect with randomly twinkling/glowing dots will give the board a premium, living feel that matches the Craftboard dark workspace aesthetic.

What:
- Replace the static Canvas dot grid with an animated starfield where a subset of dots randomly glow brighter then fade back.
- Use a TimelineView or Timer to drive the animation cycle.
- Each twinkle cycle: a random subset of dots brightens (gold/white glow) over ~1s, holds briefly, then fades back over ~1s.
- Keep the base dot grid as-is for structure.
- Vary dot sizes slightly (1–2px) for depth.
- Performance: keep draw count low — only animate a small fraction of dots per cycle (~5-10 dots visible at a time).
- No new dependencies.
notes:
Implemented the animated starfield background in KanbanBoardView.

Changes made:
- Replaced the static DotsBackground with a dynamic version using TimelineView.
- Retained the structural base dot grid for consistency.
- Introduced a SeededRNG to deterministically spawn ~24 'stars' with random coordinates, phases, speeds, and sizes so the layout is stable across view redraws.
- Animated stars using a sine wave function based on the TimelineView's date for a natural, un-synced twinkling effect.
- Applied the WorkstationTheme accent (gold) and white glows.
- Kept performance high by only rendering the glowing effect for stars currently in their bright phase (>10% brightness).

All tests passed and the build succeeded. Ready for visual review.

title: UI buat manage dependency dari Kanban app (add/remove)
description:
Why: Setelah foundation (Workstation-mqo) selesai user bisa LIHAT dependency, tapi belum bisa MANAGE dari app — masih harus balik ke CLI `bd dep add/remove`. Untuk pengalaman yang complete, perlu UI buat tambah/hapus dependency langsung dari detail view.

What:
- BeadsService.addDependency(id:, dependsOn:) → `bd dep add <id> <dep>`.
- BeadsService.removeDependency(id:, dependsOn:) → `bd dep remove <id> <dep>` (cek subcommand bd-nya dulu, mungkin `rm` atau berbeda).
- IssueDetailView: button '+ Add Blocker' yang munculkan picker (list semua open issue selain self). Pilih → call addDependency → reload.
- Per chip di 'Blocked by': tombol X kecil buat remove dependency.
- Validasi: cegah self-dependency dan cycle (bd biasanya udah handle, tapi surface error message ke user kalau gagal).

Depends on: Workstation-mqo (butuh display foundation duluan).
acceptance_criteria:
- Bisa add dependency dari detail view (picker issue → call bd dep add → reload, blocker muncul di Blocked by).
- Bisa remove dependency per chip.
- Error dari bd (mis. cycle) tampil sebagai inline error message, bukan crash.
- Tests cover service methods + happy path store mutation.
notes:
Implemented add/remove dependency UI on top of mqo foundation.

Service (Sources/BeadsWorkspace/BeadsService.swift):
- addDependency(id:, dependsOn:, in:) -> runs 'bd dep add <id> <dependsOn>' via runExpectingSuccess (no --json, no payload to decode).
- removeDependency(id:, dependsOn:, in:) -> runs 'bd dep remove <id> <dependsOn>'.
- Service-level guard rejects self-dependency and empty IDs before invoking bd.
- Cycle detection deferred to bd (default-on); errors surface as BeadsAppError.commandFailed via runExpectingSuccess.

Store (Sources/BeadsWorkspace/IssueStore.swift):
- addDependency(blockerID:, to:) / removeDependency(blockerID:, from:) wrapped in existing runMutation (auto-reload + errorMessage surface).
- After successful mutation, re-fetch selectedIssueDetail via existing private refreshSelectedDetail(expectedID:) to keep the Blocked-by chip list in sync without a full re-select.

UI (App/IssueDetailView.swift, App/BlockerPickerSheet.swift NEW):
- dependencyGroup gains optional onAddTapped + onRemove closures. 'Blocked by' passes both (read-write); 'Blocks' passes nil (read-only).
- dependencyChip splits into navigation Button + optional small X button (xmark).
- Blocked-by group always renders when selectedIssueDetail is loaded — even with zero blockers — so '+ Add' is reachable. Empty state shows 'None'.
- BlockerPickerSheet: search field + filtered list (excludes self, existing blockers, closed). Mirrors CreateIssueSheet style.

Tests (165 total, +8 from baseline 157):
- BeadsServiceTests: addDependencyPassesCorrectArgs, removeDependencyPassesCorrectArgs, addDependencyRejectsSelfDependency, addDependencyValidatesEmptyIDs, addDependencyPropagatesCLIError.
- IssueStoreTests: addDependencyTriggersReloadAndRefreshDetail, addDependencyFailureSurfacesError, removeDependencyTriggersReloadAndRefreshDetail.

Verification:
- swift test: 165/17 suites green
- swift build: clean (compiles via test run)
- xcodegen generate: ok
- ./run-app build: BUILD SUCCEEDED

Out of scope (deferred per plan):
- Editing 'Blocks' section (would require inverting perspective — different UX).
- Drag-and-drop dependency from kanban.
- Multi-select blocker add.
- Advanced picker filters (only id+title contains).
